trials.paperName: Effects of acupuncture and metformin on insulin sensitivity in women with polycystic ovary syndrome and insulin resistance: a three-armed randomized controlled trial
trials.primaryOutcome: Change in HOMA-IR from baseline to 4 months after baseline visit
trials.trialSuccess: No(acupuncture was not more effective than metformin or sham acupuncture)
trials.statisticalModel: Mann–Whitney U test Kolmogorov-Smirnov test Fisher's exact test
trials.treatmentLevels: Metformin + Sham Acupuncture; Sham Acupuncture + Placebo; True Acupuncture + Placebo

trials.paperName: Ivermectin in combination with doxycycline for treating COVID-19 symptoms: a randomized trial
trials.primaryOutcome: Time (days) from treatment initiation (day 1) to clinical recovery
trials.trialSuccess: Yes
trials.statisticalModel: Primary: Kaplan–Meier survival curves + log-rank test; Cox proportional hazards regression for hazard ratios Secondary: Logistic regression for categorical recovery time and disease progression

trials.paperName: Electronic health record alerts for acute kidney injury: multicenter, randomized clinical trial
trials.primaryOutcome: Composite of AKI progression (to a higher KDIGO stage), receipt of dialysis, or death within 14 days of randomization
trials.trialSuccess: No
trials.statisticalModel: Primary analysis: Mantel–Haenszel test (stratified by hospital) for relative risks; Cox proportional hazards regression (stratified by hospital) for Time-to-event Sensitivity: modified Poisson generalized estimating equations with robust variance Continuous/process measures: Van Elteren test (stratified); Kaplan–Meier curves and log-rank tests for site-specific Time-to-event analyses

trials.paperName: Effect of vitamin D supplementation in patients with chronic hepatitis C after direct-acting antiviral treatment: a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial
trials.primaryOutcome: Change from baseline to week 6 in serum fibrogenesis markers: TGF-β1, TIMP-1, MMP-9, P3NP
trials.trialSuccess: No
trials.statisticalModel: Primary: independent two-sample t-tests comparing mean change from baseline between arms Sensitivity: median quantile regression of change scores Baseline balance: t-tests or chi-square/Fisher’s exact tests

trials.paperName: A randomized clinical trial assessing the effect of automated medication-targeted alerts on acute kidney injury outcomes
trials.primaryOutcome: Composite of AKI progression (to a higher KDIGO stage), receipt of dialysis, or death within 14 days of randomization or hospital discharge
trials.trialSuccess: No
trials.statisticalModel: Primary: Cochran–Mantel–Haenszel χ² test stratified by hospital for relative risks Continuous/process: Van Elteren test (stratified by hospital) Subgroup interactions: binomial regression model adjusted for hospital (interaction p-values) Mediation: product-of-coefficients method with bootstrapped 95% CIs, adjusting for key baseline covariates

trials.paperName: The Effect of Sitagliptin on Carotid Artery Atherosclerosis in Type 2 Diabetes: The PROLOGUE Randomized Controlled Trial
trials.primaryOutcome: Percentage change in mean common carotid artery intima-media thickness (CCA IMT) at 24 months after randomization
trials.trialSuccess: No
trials.statisticalModel: Primary & secondary: baseline-adjusted ANCOVA Sensitivity: mixed-effects model for repeated measures Baseline comparisons: unpaired t-tests and Fisher’s exact tests

trials.paperName: Comparing Two Methods of Delivering ThinkRx Cognitive Training to Children Ages 8–14: a Randomized Controlled Trial of Equivalency
trials.primaryOutcome: Change scores (post – pre) on eight Woodcock–Johnson III cognitive subtests: associative memory, visual processing, auditory processing, logic & reasoning, processing speed, working memory/attention, long-term memory, and general intellectual ability.
trials.trialSuccess: Equivalence (no significant difference) demonstrated on 7 of 8 measures; however, long-term memory gains were significantly greater in the ThinkRx arm
trials.statisticalModel: Primary analysis: MANOVA on difference scores with Bonferroni correction Sensitivity: ANCOVA for each post-test controlling baseline; OLS regression with clustered standard errors (for sibling dependence)
trials.randomizationScheme: Blocked randomization, stratified by sibling status

trials.paperName: Evaluation of Ivermectin as a Potential Treatment for Mild to Moderate COVID-19: A Double-BlindRandomized Placebo Controlled Trialin Eastern India
trials.primaryOutcome: Proportion of patients with a negative SARS-CoV-2 RT-PCR on Day 6 post-enrolment
trials.trialSuccess: No
trials.statisticalModel: Baseline comparisons: independent-samples Student’s t-test (continuous) and chi-square test (categorical) Outcome comparisons: rate ratios with chi-square tests

trials.paperName: Umbilical vein oxytocin for the treatment of retained placenta (Release Study): a double-blind, randomised controlled trial
trials.primaryOutcome: Need for manual removal of the placenta after randomization
trials.trialSuccess: No
trials.statisticalModel: Primary & binary secondary outcomes: relative risks with χ² tests Continuous skewed outcomes (e.g., time): Mann-Whitney tests Subgroup consistency: Mantel–Haenszel homogeneity tests

trials.paperName: Influence of videolaryngoscopy using McGrath Mac on the need for a helper to perform intubation during general anaesthesia: a multicentre randomised video-no-video trial
trials.primaryOutcome: Proportion of orotracheal intubations requiring assistance at the operator’s request
trials.trialSuccess: No
trials.statisticalModel: Primary outcome (binary): comparison of proportions by χ² test (with Bonferroni correction for multiple comparisons when assessing IDS components). Secondary categorical outcomes: χ² or Fisher’s exact tests (as appropriate). Continuous outcomes (e.g., POGO, time): Wilcoxon rank-sum test (after verifying normality with Shapiro–Wilk). Intubation Difficulty Scale (ordinal): Wilcoxon or χ² for class comparisons. Hemodynamic changes: pre- vs post-intubation medians compared by Wilcoxon; between-group differences tested similarly.

trials.paperName: Chlorhexidine oral rinses for symptomatic COPD: a randomised, blind, placebo-controlled preliminary study
trials.primaryOutcome: Change from baseline to 8 weeks in oral and sputum microbiota biomass (log₁₀ 16S rRNA copy number)
trials.trialSuccess: No
trials.statisticalModel: Primary analysis: Two-sample t-test on log10-transformed biomass data, with multiple imputation for missing sputum weights Secondary analyses: Linear regression models for biodiversity indices, BCSS, SGRQ and inflammatory markers, with each model adjusted for the baseline value of the measure

trials.paperName: Effect of an Intensive Food-as-Medicine Program on Health and Health Care Use
trials.primaryOutcome: Hemoglobin A1c (HbA1c) level at 6 months from enrollment
trials.trialSuccess: No
trials.statisticalModel: Primary and secondary outcomes: Mixed-effects models (longitudinal models) for laboratory test results and health care use outcomes Linear regression models for program engagement and survey outcomes at 6 and 12 months Models included heteroskedasticity-robust, participant-clustered standard errors Multiple imputation for missing baseline measures (except HbA1c, which was complete) Inverse covariance weighting for creating index scores from multiple survey measures
trials.randomizationScheme: Stratified by site and hba1c category

trials.paperName: Does route matter? Impact of route of oxytocin administration on postpartum bleeding: A double-blind, randomized controlled trial
trials.primaryOutcome: Proportion with Blood Loss ≥ 500 Mean Blood Loss
trials.primaryOutcomeType: Proportion with Blood Loss ≥ 500: Binary Mean Blood Loss: Continuous
trials.trialSuccess: No
trials.statisticalModel: Categorical variables: Pearson χ² or Fisher’s exact tests Continuous variables: Independent t-tests or Mann-Whitney U tests (depending on normality)

trials.paperName: A telerehabilitation programme in post-discharge COVID-19 patients (TERECO): a randomised controlled trial
trials.primaryOutcome: 6-minute walking distance (6MWD) in meters at 6 weeks (post-treatment) and at follow-up (~28 weeks)
trials.trialSuccess: Yes
trials.statisticalModel: For primary outcome and most secondary continuous outcomes: Constrained longitudinal data analysis (linear mixed effects model with equality constraint on baseline means), adjusted for center (fixed effect) with random intercept for participants For mMRC-dyspnoea: Generalized linear model of the Poisson family with a log link, adjusted for center and using natural logarithm of the number of observed occasions as offset, with cluster robust standard errors

trials.paperName: The effect of Snoezelen intervention on problem behaviors in children with cerebral palsy: A randomized controlled trial
trials.primaryOutcome: Change in Child Behavior Checklist (CBCL 1.5–5) scores (pre- vs. post-intervention)
trials.trialSuccess: Yes
trials.statisticalModel: Within-group: Wilcoxon signed-rank test Between-group: Mann-Whitney U test Correlation: Spearman’s rank correlation (ρ) Association: Linear regression (R² for GMFCS vs. change in emotional reactivity)

trials.paperName: Internet-delivered therapist-guided physical activity for mild to moderate depression: a randomized controlled trial
trials.primaryOutcome: Reduction in depressive symptoms as measured by the Beck Depression Inventory-II (BDI-II)
trials.trialSuccess: Yes
trials.statisticalModel: Two-way mixed ANOVA (group × time)

trials.paperName: The Effectiveness and Safety of Intensive Lipid-Lowering with Different Rosuvastatin-Based Regimens in Patients at High Cardiovascular Disease Risk: A Nonblind, Randomized, Controlled Trial
trials.primaryOutcome: Change in lipid levels (total cholesterol and LDL-C) from baseline at 24 weeks
trials.trialSuccess: Yes, partially. At 24 weeks LDL-C and TC were significantly lower in statin + PCSK9-i and combine3 vs statin or statin + ezetimibe (overall p < 0.05); combine3 and PCSK9 arms met superiority, while adding ezetimibe alone did not.
trials.statisticalModel: Within-group changes: Wilcoxon signed-rank test with rank-biserial correlation Between-group comparisons: Kruskal–Wallis one-way ANOVA with Dunn’s post-hoc test Target-achievement analysis: Multivariate logistic regression (adjusting for covariates)

trials.paperName: The Kanyakla study: Randomized controlled trial of a microclinic social network intervention for promoting engagement and retention in HIV care in rural western Kenya
trials.primaryOutcome: Time to disengagement: first ≥90-day absence from any HIV care visit in 12 months Time in care: proportion of time adherent to appointments over 12 months
trials.primaryOutcomeType: Time to disengagement: Time-to-event Time in care: Continuous proportion
trials.trialSuccess: No
trials.statisticalModel: Disengagement: Cox proportional hazards Time in Care: linear regression with bootstrapping Continuous outcomes (social support, stigma): linear regression with 10,000-replication bootstrap, adjusting for baseline. Disclosure counts: Poisson regression, adjusting for baseline, with robust SE for clustering

trials.paperName: Prospective Randomized Trial Comparing Hepatic Venous Outflow and Renal Function after Conventional versus Piggyback Liver Transplantation
trials.primaryOutcome: Hepatic venous outflow drainage, quantified by the free hepatic venous pressure (FHVP) to central venous pressure (CVP) gradient
trials.trialSuccess: No
trials.statisticalModel: Primary outcome: Student’s t-test / ANOVA (or Mann–Whitney if non-normal), plus χ² or Fisher’s exact for categorical comparisons Secondary outcomes: Generalized Estimating Equations (GEE) for longitudinal serum creatinine and for binary severe ARF; Kaplan–Meier curves with log-rank test for survival analysis

trials.paperName: Effect of ecological momentary assessment, goal-setting and personalized phone-calls on adherence to interval walking training using the InterWalk application among patients with type 2 diabetes—A pilot randomized controlled trial
trials.primaryOutcome: Adherence to interval walking training (IWT) measured as total accumulated minutes of IWT during the 12-week intervention period
trials.trialSuccess: No(ITT analysis)
trials.statisticalModel: Primary outcome: Mixed effects model with fixed effects for group, age, and sex Secondary outcomes: Mixed linear models with same fixed factors as for primary outcome, additionally adjusted for respective baseline values

trials.paperName: Computer-aided X-ray screening for tuberculosis and HIV testing among adults with cough in Malawi (the PROSPECT study): A randomised trial and cost-effectiveness analysis
trials.primaryOutcome: Time in days (up to day 56) to TB treatment initiation
trials.trialSuccess: Yes Compared to SOC, the HIV-TB screening arm significantly shortened time to TB treatment (HR 2.86; 95% CI 1.04–7.87; p = 0.04). The HIV-only arm did not differ significantly (HR 1.51; 95% CI 0.49–4.62)
trials.statisticalModel: Primary outcome: log-rank test and unadjusted Cox proportional-hazards models Secondary binary outcomes: binomial regression with log link (risk ratios)

trials.paperName: Development and First Phase Evaluation of a Maternity Leave Educational Tool for Pregnant, Working Women in California
trials.primaryOutcome: Proportion of women taking more than one week of prenatal leave (among those delivering ≥37 weeks)
trials.trialSuccess: No
trials.statisticalModel: Descriptive comparisons used t-tests for continuous outcomes and chi-square or Fisher’s exact tests for categorical outcomes

trials.paperName: Can recombinant human thrombomodulin increase survival among patients with severe septicinduced disseminated intravascular coagulation: a single-centre, open-label, randomised controlled trial
trials.primaryOutcome: 28-day and 90-day survival rates
trials.trialSuccess: No
trials.statisticalModel: Kaplan–Meier curves with log-rank tests Mann-Whitney test Fisher’s exact test

trials.paperName: Auriculotherapy in the prevention of postoperative urinary retention in patients with thoracotomy and thoracic epidural analgesia A randomized, double-blinded trial
trials.primaryOutcome: Requirement for bladder catheterization during the day and the first night following surgery
trials.trialSuccess: Yes
trials.statisticalModel: Primary outcome: Fisher exact test for proportion comparison; Kaplan–Meier survival analysis with log-rank test for time to catheterization. Secondary analyses: Univariate tests including Chi-square/Fisher exact for categorical variables, Student’s t-test or Mann–Whitney for continuous variables, Hodges–Lehmann estimator and Cohen’s d for effect sizes, and Mann–Whitney for anxiety/comfort scores.

trials.paperName: Multi-strain probiotics (Hexbio) containing MCP BCMC strains improved constipation and gut motility in Parkinson’s disease: A randomised controlled trial
trials.primaryOutcome: Change in constipation symptoms assessed by the 9-item Garrigues Questionnaire (GQ) Bowel opening frequency (BOF) per week (mean weekly bowel movements) Whole gut transit time (GTT) in hours
trials.primaryOutcomeType: GQ items: Ordinal BOF (BM/week): Continuous GTT: Continuous
trials.trialSuccess: Yes
trials.statisticalModel: Between-group comparisons: univariate tests (t-test or Mann–Whitney U for continuous/count data; χ² or Fisher’s exact for categorical outcomes) Regression analyses: generalized linear models—ordinal logistic regression for GQ items, binary logistic for BOF categories, linear regression for BM/week and GTT Within-group analyses: paired t-tests or Wilcoxon signed-rank tests

trials.paperName: Mobile phone reminders and peer counseling improve adherence and treatment outcomes of patients on ART in Malaysia: A randomized clinical trial
trials.primaryOutcome: Adherence (improved scheduled clinic attendance and medication adherence self-report), attendance
trials.primaryOutcomeType: Delta adherence: Continuous Adherence: Categorical Attendance: Categorical
trials.trialSuccess: Yes
trials.statisticalModel: Parametric tests: Student’s t-test, two-way repeated-measures ANOVA for adherence, CD4, viral load, and weight Nonparametric tests: Chi-square/Fisher’s exact tests for categorical comparisons; Friedman’s ANOVA for within-group non-normal data Multivariate models: mixed repeated-measures ANCOVA adjusting for baseline covariates (age, ethnicity, education, income)

trials.paperName: Impact of the modality of mechanical ventilation on bleeding during pituitary surgery A single blinded randomized trial
trials.primaryOutcome: Intraoperative bleeding during transsphenoidal pituitary surgery, scored using a 7-level bleeding scale.
trials.trialSuccess: No
trials.statisticalModel: Primary outcome: ordinal logistic regression (proportional cumulative odds) Secondary outcomes: Student’s t-test or Mann–Whitney for continuous variables; Chi-square or Fisher’s exact for categorical variables

trials.paperName: Effectiveness of community-based health education and home support program to reduce blood pressure among patients with uncontrolled hypertension in Nepal: A cluster-randomized trial
trials.primaryOutcome: Proportion of participants with controlled systolic blood pressure (SBP < 140 mmHg) at six months
trials.trialSuccess: Yes
trials.statisticalModel: Comparisons: independent t-tests for continuous variables; χ² or Fisher’s exact for categorical Primary/secondary binary outcomes: binary logistic regression (adjusted for covariates & clustering) Knowledge score: linear regression of follow-up score on baseline score and intervention

trials.paperName: Adjunctive sertraline in HIV-associated cryptococcal meningitis: a randomised, placebo-controlled, double-blind phase 3 trial
trials.primaryOutcome: 18-week all-cause mortality (survival)
trials.trialSuccess: No
trials.statisticalModel: Primary: Kaplan-Meier survival curves and Cox proportional hazards regression (intention-to-treat) 29 Secondary: Linear regression and mixed-effects regression (random intercept) for early fungicidal activity Cumulative incidence functions (competing risks), logistic regression, Fisher’s exact tests, general linear models, and Wilcoxon rank-sum tests for other endpoints
trials.randomizationScheme: blocked randomization, stratified by site and ART status

trials.paperName: A randomized controlled safety and feasibility trial of floatation-REST in anxious and depressed individuals
trials.primaryOutcome: Feasibility, assessed by adherence to six sessions of the assigned intervention (target ≥ 80%).
trials.trialSuccess: Adherence met the ≥80% feasibility threshold for Pool-REST (85%) and Pool-REST Preferred (89%). Chair-REST did not meet the threshold (74%).
trials.statisticalModel: Feasibility (credibility/expectancy): One-way ANOVA Tolerability (dropout): Kaplan–Meier survival analysis with log-rank tests Session duration & event ratings: Linear mixed‐effects models (REML, AR1 covariance), with fixed effects of condition, visit (and event for safety), and their interactions, random intercepts for subject ID

trials.paperName: Influenza Hemagglutination-inhibition Antibody Titer as a Mediator of Vaccine-induced Protection for Influenza B
trials.primaryOutcome: Time to laboratory-confirmed influenza B/Victoria infection
trials.trialSuccess: Yes
trials.statisticalModel: Cox models for total, direct, and indirect (mediated) effects. Logistic regression to create inverse-odds-ratio weights for mediation. Bootstrap (10 000 resamples) for CIs.

trials.paperName: Nasal ventilation and rapid maxillary expansion (RME): a randomized trial
trials.primaryOutcome: pressure, velocity
trials.trialSuccess: Partially, No statistically significant differences among groups in nasal pressure or velocity (P = 0.17, P = 0.08). However, Hybrid-Hyrax showed a trend of greater improvement and was statistically superior to Hyrax in adjusted regressions for maximum velocity (P = 0.03) and nasal obstruction resolution (P = 0.04).
trials.statisticalModel: Linear regression (with transformed outcomes) for continuous variables Fisher’s exact test for categorical variables (e.g., obstruction resolution) Adjusted multivariable regression controlling for confounders Multivariable regularized logistic regression (penalized) for predicting nasal obstruction resolution

trials.paperName: Reducing therapeutic misconception: A randomized intervention trial in hypothetical clinical trials
trials.primaryOutcome: Therapeutic misconception, measured by the 10-item Therapeutic Misconception Scale (score range 10–50).
trials.trialSuccess: Yes
trials.statisticalModel: Unadjusted two-tailed t-test for TM score comparison Chi-square test for willingness to participate Factorial two-way ANOVAs (group × baseline characteristics) to assess main and interaction effects, including education

trials.paperName: A randomized synbiotic trial to prevent sepsis among infants in rural India
trials.primaryOutcome: Composite of death or neonatal sepsis (culture-positive/negative sepsis or lower respiratory tract infection) within 60 days.
trials.trialSuccess: Yes
trials.statisticalModel: Primary and secondary binary outcomes: Chi-square or Fisher’s exact tests to compare proportions; risk ratios with 95% CIs Weight gain comparison: Two-sample t-test (Satterthwaite)

trials.paperName: Exercise Training and Weight Gain in Obese Pregnant Women: A Randomized Controlled Trial (ETIP Trial)
trials.primaryOutcome: Gestational weight gain (GWG) from baseline (week 12–18) to delivery
trials.trialSuccess: No
trials.statisticalModel: Continuous outcomes: linear mixed models (time × treatment fixed; participant random) Binary outcomes: mixed-effects logistic regression – Analyses by intention-to-treat; per-protocol sensitivity analyses also performed

trials.paperName: Targeting brain health in subjective cognitive decline: insights from a multidomain randomized controlled trial
trials.primaryOutcome: One-year change in cognitive function: Rey Auditory Verbal Learning Test (RAVLT) z-standardized composite scores for memory, attention-executive, and visuospatial domains
trials.trialSuccess: Memory composite, RAVLT total, visuospatial: No Attention-executive: Yes (MI vs ACI)
trials.statisticalModel: Primary & secondary: Complete-case ANCOVA (post-intervention measure ~ group + baseline + age + sex + education), with Bonferroni-adjusted pairwise t-tests 36. Functional connectivity: MANCOVAN followed by voxel-wise univariate tests (FDR-corrected). Exploratory brain–behavior: Linear mixed-effects models.

trials.paperName: A Randomized Trial of Rectal Indomethacin to Prevent Post-ERCP Pancreatitis
trials.primaryOutcome: Incidence of post-ERCP pancreatitis
trials.trialSuccess: Yes
trials.statisticalModel: Primary & moderate-to-severe outcomes: Two-tailed Fisher’s exact test (α=0.041, O’Brien–Fleming spending) 37 Hospital stay: Kruskal–Wallis rank test 37 Subgroup interactions: Multiple logistic regression with interaction terms

trials.paperName: A trial comparing nucleoside monotherapy with combination therapy in HIV-infected adults with CD4 cell counts from 200 to 500 per cubic millimeter. AIDS Clinical Trials Group Study 175 Study Team
trials.primaryOutcome: Time to a composite of: ≥ 50 % decline in CD4 count Development of AIDS (CDC-defined) Death
trials.trialSuccess: Yes
trials.statisticalModel: Kaplan–Meier estimates + log-rank tests Cox proportional-hazards models stratified on prior therapy (with subgroup–treatment interaction) ANOVA and two-sample t-tests for CD4 count changes
trials.randomizationScheme: stratified by prior antiretroviral exposure, block

trials.paperName: Linking alcohol- and drug-dependent adults to primary medical care: a randomized controlled trial of a multi-disciplinary health intervention in a detoxification unit
trials.primaryOutcome: Time to first self-reported linkage (≥1 visit) to a primary care MD/NP/PA within 12 months
trials.trialSuccess: Yes
trials.statisticalModel: Primary: log-rank test; Cox proportional-hazards model for HR Secondary: generalized linear mixed models (PROC MIXED with unstructured covariance) for continuous outcomes; Wilcoxon rank-sum for utilization rates
trials.randomizationScheme: blocked randomization, stratified by primary substance (alcohol vs drugs)

trials.paperName: A Randomized Comparison Between the Pentax AWS Video Laryngoscope and the Macintosh Laryngoscope in Morbidly Obese Patients
trials.primaryOutcome: Time to successful tracheal intubation—measured from blade insertion to capnographic confirmation, censored at 100 seconds if exceeded
trials.trialSuccess: No
trials.statisticalModel: Primary outcome: Cox proportional‐hazards regression (adjusted for ASA and Mallampati), Kaplan–Meier curves with 95% confidence bands Secondary outcomes: Wilcoxon rank‐sum test for number of attempts Fisher’s exact test or logistic regression for binary outcomes (first‐attempt and overall success, bleeding, glottic view) Analysis of covariance for ease‐of‐intubation score Proportional‐odds logistic regression for sore‐throat severity

trials.paperName: A Randomized, Double-Blind Comparison of Licorice Versus Sugar-Water Gargle for Prevention of Postoperative Sore Throat and Postextubation Coughing
trials.primaryOutcome: Incidence of postoperative sore throat at rest, assessed across the first 4 postextubation hours (at 0.5, 1.5, and 4 h)
trials.trialSuccess: Yes
trials.statisticalModel: Primary outcome: Modified Mantel–Haenszel test adjusting for within-patient correlation Pain‐score trajectories: Linear mixed-effects model with autoregressive covariance Coughing (ordinal): GEE with cumulative-logit link Individual binary comparisons: χ² tests (with Bonferroni correction)

trials.paperName: Combined Versus Sequential Injection of Mepivacaine and Ropivacaine for Supraclavicular Nerve Blocks
trials.primaryOutcome: Time (minutes) from completion of injection to onset of sensory block in all four nerve distributions (median, ulnar, radial, musculocutaneous)
trials.trialSuccess: No
trials.statisticalModel: Primary: Kaplan–Meier with log-rank test (stratified by sex) and multivariable Cox regression adjusting for sex; sensitivity via Wilcoxon test 42 Secondary: Time-to-event outcomes: Kaplan–Meier/log-rank and Cox (as above) 42 Pain scores & opioid use: ANCOVA on VRS and log-transformed opioid consumption adjusting for sex

trials.paperName: Treatment of Periodontal Disease and the Risk of Preterm Birth
trials.primaryOutcome: Gestational age at the end of pregnancy
trials.trialSuccess: No
trials.statisticalModel: Primary: Kaplan–Meier curves and log-rank test stratified by center; Cox proportional-hazards regression Competing-risks: live birth vs spontaneous abortion/stillbirth Secondary: t-tests (birth weight), Fisher’s exact or chi-square (proportions), Wilcoxon signed-rank (Apgar), linear mixed models for periodontal measures

trials.paperName: Influence of needle-insertion depth on epidural spread and clinical outcomes in caudal epidural injections: a randomized clinical trial
trials.primaryOutcome: Success rate of epidural spread without intravascular uptake (binary per patient)
trials.trialSuccess: Yes
trials.statisticalModel: Primary outcome: Chi-square test Continuous outcomes (pain scores): Student’s t-test Ordinal outcome (PGIC): Mann–Whitney U test Normality check: Shapiro–Wilk test

trials.paperName: Impact of a randomized controlled trial of discounts on fruits, vegetables, and noncaloric beverages in NYC supermarkets on food intake and health risk factors
trials.primaryOutcome: Change in grams per day of: Fruit intake Vegetable intake Non‐caloric beverage intake (diet soda, bottled water, seltzer)
trials.statisticalModel: Multiple linear regression of mid‐intervention values on group, adjusting for baseline and COVID-19 covariate; two‐tailed P<0.05 for significance; Cohen’s f² for effect sizes

trials.paperName: Evaluating the effectiveness of a structured, simulator-assisted, peer-led training on cardiovascular physical examination in third-year medical students: a prospective, randomized, controlled trial
trials.primaryOutcome: Total score (0–25) on a standardized cardiovascular-PE checklist
trials.trialSuccess: Yes
trials.statisticalModel: Normality check: Shapiro–Wilk Group comparisons: Independent t-test (for normal data) or Mann–Whitney U (non-normal), with Bonferroni correction for subdomains; Fisher’s exact for nominal Correlations: Spearman’s rs Adjusted analysis: Hierarchical multiple linear regression of total score on prespecified covariates, then group assignment

trials.paperName: Comparison between high-flow nasal cannula (HFNC) therapy and noninvasive ventilation (NIV) in children with acute respiratory failure by bronchiolitis: a randomized controlled trial
trials.primaryOutcome: Requirement for invasive mechanical ventilation
trials.trialSuccess: Yes
trials.statisticalModel: Primary outcome: Farrington-Manning test for noninferiority 47 Group comparisons: Wilcoxon/Mann–Whitney test; chi-square test 47 Time-to-event (intubation): Log-rank test 47 Repeated measures: Friedman test with Wilcoxon paired post-hoc (Bonferroni correction) 47 Effect size: Cohen’s d

trials.paperName: The effectiveness of breakfast recommendations on weight loss: a randomized controlled trial
trials.journal: The American journal of clinical nutrition
trials.primaryOutcome: Weight change from baseline to 16 weeks
trials.trialSuccess: No
trials.statisticalModel: Primary analysis: Ordinary least squares linear regression, adjusted for baseline covariates. Type II ANOVA for between-group comparisons. Multiple imputation for missing data (via MICE package in R). Secondary analyses: Compliance-adjusted regression using self-reported adherence.

trials.paperName: Impact of a pre-feeding oral stimulation program on first feed attempt in preterm infants: Double-blind controlled clinical trial
trials.primaryOutcome: Oral Feeding Skills (OFS) level at first oral feeding, which includes: Proficiency (PRO, % volume taken in first 5 minutes) Rate of Transfer (RT, mL/min)
trials.trialSuccess: Yes
trials.statisticalModel: Fisher’s exact test, Student’s t-test, Mann–Whitney U test Logistic regression (OFS level 4 vs. lower levels) Generalized estimating equations (GEE) for volume intake trends Cox proportional hazards model for time to full oral feeding
trials.randomizationScheme: blocked randomization, stratified by gestational age

trials.paperName: A controlled trial of interferon gamma to prevent infection in chronic granulomatous disease
trials.primaryOutcome: Time to first serious infection (requiring hospitalisation + parenteral antibiotics)
trials.trialSuccess: Yes
trials.statisticalModel: Time-to-event: Kaplan–Meier curves with log-rank test (for time to first serious infection). Binary/count outcomes: χ² or Fisher’s exact tests for proportions; comparisons of infection counts (likely Poisson or similar, though not specified)

trials.paperName: The impact of sleep hygiene education and lavender essential oil inhalation on the sleep quality and overall well-being of athletes who undergo late-evening training: a randomized controlled trial
trials.primaryOutcome: Sleep latency over 7-day intervention
trials.trialSuccess: Yes for sleep latency group comparison
trials.statisticalModel: One-way ANOVA/Kruskal-Wallis for actigraphy; two-way ANOVA or F1-LD-F1 model for repeated questionnaire outcomes
trials.treatmentLevels: Control; Lavender oil; Sleep hygiene; Sleep hygiene + lavender oil

trials.paperName: Efficacy of tenapanor in managing hyperphosphatemia and constipation in hemodialysis patients: A randomized controlled trial
trials.primaryOutcome: Change in serum phosphorus at week 23 and change in BSFS at week 7
trials.trialSuccess: Mixed; BSFS improved within tenapanor group, serum phosphorus between-group difference not significant
trials.statisticalModel: Repeated-measures ANOVA for blood data; Sankey/McNemar with Bonferroni for BSFS/laxative changes; ITT

trials.paperName: Analgesic Effect of Ultrasound-Guided Anterior Quadratus Lumborum Block at the L2 Level in Patients Undergoing Laparoscopic Partial Nephrectomy: A Single-Center, Randomized Controlled Trial
trials.primaryOutcome: Total perioperative cumulative sufentanil consumption
trials.trialSuccess: Yes
trials.statisticalModel: Independent/paired t-tests or Mann-Whitney/Wilcoxon; chi-square/Fisher exact for categorical outcomes

trials.paperName: Effect of Vaginal Spheres and Pelvic Floor Exercises on Female Sexual Function in Women with Urinary Incontinence: A Randomized Controlled Trial (Secondary Analysis)
trials.primaryOutcome: FSFI total score at end of treatment
trials.trialSuccess: No significant overall FSFI total between-group difference; desire domain significant
trials.statisticalModel: Mann-Whitney U tests; per-protocol primary analysis and ITT with multiple imputation sensitivity analysis
trials.treatmentLevels: Pelvic floor training; Vaginal spheres + pelvic floor training

trials.paperName: Taking Charge After Acute Exacerbation of Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease: A Randomized Controlled Feasibility Trial of a Psychologically Informed Self-Management Intervention
trials.primaryOutcome: Frequency of moderate or severe AECOPD within 12 months
trials.trialSuccess: No significant primary rate difference
trials.statisticalModel: Poisson regression with log observation time offset

trials.paperName: Transcranial direct current stimulation and attention skills in burnout patients: a randomized blinded sham-controlled pilot study
trials.primaryOutcome: RBANS Attention Index after 3 weeks
trials.trialSuccess: Yes for tDCS-by-time interaction on attention
trials.statisticalModel: Linear mixed model with fixed tDCS, time, and interaction effects and participant random effect

trials.paperName: Functional imagery training versus motivational interviewing for weight loss: a randomised controlled trial of brief individual interventions for overweight and obesity
trials.primaryOutcome: Weight loss and waist-circumference reduction at 6 and 12 months
trials.trialSuccess: Yes; FIT had greater weight and waist-circumference reductions than MI
trials.statisticalModel: Linear mixed-effects models for repeated weight and waist outcomes; Bayesian sensitivity models in supplement

trials.paperName: Transcranial direct current stimulation and cognitive training in the treatment of cognitive deficit in schizophrenia: a randomized controlled trial
trials.primaryOutcome: Change in RBANS Total Score at 1 week
